data.title: Strawberry season starting in Europe, with early March harvest of Gariguette variety. Producers recommend storing in fridge.
data.description: In Europe, the Gariguette strawberries are beginning to appear on shelves as winter comes to an end. Known as the queen of French strawberries, these berries are harvested starting in March. The Gariguette strawberries are celebrated for their sweet and tangy taste, along with their rich aroma, elongated shape, and vibrant red color. European producers recommend placing them at the top of the shopping basket and storing them in the crisper drawer of the refrigerator for optimal preservation.

data.title: New website launched by Kyushu Volcano Tourism Council for travel partners, showcasing stunning volcanic landscapes.
data.description: In Nagasaki, Kumamoto, and Kagoshima, Japan, visitors can experience the unique lifestyle shaped by active volcanoes. Located on the island of Kyushu, each season offers a distinct charm, with a variety of leisure and cultural activities available, showcasing the beauty of the region from the mountains to the sea. Travelers can embark on an extraordinary journey filled with unexpected and meaningful encounters. Kyushu boasts remarkable volcanic terrains and tranquil "satoyama" rural settlements where communities coexist harmoniously with nature. Explore this captivating destination for a one-of-a-kind experience.

data.title: In Feb 2026, BOSS Zhipin repurchased 410,642 shares using near RMB25M, totaling over RMB340M this year. Authorization allows repurchases up to USD250M till Aug 2026.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, KANZHUN LIMITED, also known as "BOSS Zhipin" (Nasdaq: BZ; HK: 2076), disclosed the latest update on its share repurchase initiative. The Company utilized roughly RMB25 million to buy back 410,642 common shares on both February 17 and February 18. Over the course of 2026, KANZHUN LIMITED has allocated over RMB340 million towards repurchasing shares, demonstrating a dedication to enhancing shareholder value. The Company still has authorization within its current program to buy back up to USD250 million worth of shares, which will be active until the end of August 2026.

data.title: Garrett Motion to produce breakthrough E-Cooling Compressor for electric buses & trucks in China starting in 2027.
data.description: Cling, a prominent company in integrating HVAC systems for trucks and buses, has given Garrett the contract for mass-producing its innovative oil-free centrifugal compressor with foil bearings. Production is scheduled to commence in 2027. This partnership signifies a significant development in the industry, as the two companies collaborate to bring this advanced technology to market. The compressor is expected to revolutionize the efficiency and reliability of HVAC systems in the truck and bus sector, with a substantial impact on the market once it enters production.

data.title: Acceleronix and IDEMIA launch IFPP Early Access Program at MWC26 Barcelona with Tele2 IoT. Leaders in IoT and payment solutions.
data.description: A partnership between Acceleronix and IDEMIA Secure Transactions has been forged in Barcelona, Spain on Feb. 20, 2026. Acceleronix is known globally for its IoT solutions, while IDEMIA Secure Transactions specializes in payment and connectivity solutions. The duo is introducing the In-Factory Profile Provisioning (IFPP) Early Access Program. This initiative marks a significant step in advancing IoT capabilities and will impact the industry greatly.

data.title: Insulin delivery devices market to reach USD 49.23 billion by 2031, 7.82% CAGR driven by digital pens and pumps.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, as of February 20, 2026, the insulin delivery devices market is projected to be worth USD 33.79 billion. By 2031, this figure is predicted to increase to USD 49.23 billion, indicating a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.82% throughout the forecast period.

data.title: Dow drops over 250 points amid US-Iran tensions, fear index remains high. Deere & Co. jumps 11.6%, Walmart falls 1.4%, Booking Holdings drops 6.2%. US trade deficit rises to $70.3 billion in December.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index reported a rise in fear levels and remained in the "Fear" zone on Thursday. In the U.S. stock market, the Dow Jones index experienced a drop of over 250 points due to President Donald Trump's ambiguous stance on potential military action in Iran. Deere & Co. (NYSE: DE) saw an 11.6% increase after posting positive quarterly earnings, while Walmart Inc. (NASDAQ: WMT) declined by 1.4% despite meeting expectations. Booking Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: BKNG) also fell by 6.2% following its fourth-quarter results. Economic data revealed an increase in the U.S. trade deficit to $70.3 billion in December, compared to $53 billion in the previous month. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
